{`)b
Script which takes one or more file paths and reports on their detected
encodings
Example::
    % chardetect somefile someotherfile
    somefile: windows-1252 with confidence 0.5
    someotherfile: ascii with confidence 1.0
If no paths are provided, it takes its input from stdin.
__version__)
UniversalDetector
stdinc
    Return a string describing the probable encoding of a file or
    list of strings.
    :param lines: The lines to get the encoding of.
    :type lines: Iterable of bytes
    :param name: Name of file or collection of lines
    :type name: str
    
encodingz
 with confidence Z
confidencez
: no result)
	bytearrayZ
feedZ
done
close
result)
lines
name
liner	
:/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/chardet/cli/chardetect.py
description_of
    Handles command line arguments and gets things started.
    :param argv: List of arguments, as if specified on the command-line.
                 If None, ``sys.argv[1:]`` is used instead.
    :type argv: list of str
    zVTakes one or more file paths and reports their detected                      encodings)
description
inputz^File whose encoding we would like to determine.                               (default: stdin)
help
type
nargs
defaultz	--version
versionz	%(prog)s )
actionr
You are running chardetect interactively. Press CTRL-D twice at the start of a blank line to signal the end of your input. If you want help, run chardetect --help
